# **V10 ARCHITECTURE AUDIT — HOSTILE VERIFICATION REPORT**

## **VRAM Verification**

1. **Model Weights:** Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct-AWQ (4-bit). 32.5 billion parameters × 4 bits / 8 bits per byte \= 16.25 GB. With quantization group overhead, \~18 GB is accurate.1  
2. **KV Cache @ 32K context:** 64 layers × 8 KV heads × 128 head dimension × 2 (K+V) × 2 bytes (FP16) × 32,768 tokens \= 8,589,934,592 bytes \= \~8.59 GB. Claim of \~8 GB is accurate.1  
3. **Total:** 16.25 GB (Weights) \+ 8.59 GB (KV Cache) \+ 2.0 GB (vLLM overhead) \+ 2.0 GB (BGE-M3) \+ 0.5 GB (TEI overhead) \= 29.34 GB.  
4. This footprint securely fits within a 48 GB VRAM allocation (e.g., dual 24GB or single 48GB GPU), maintaining approximately 18 GB of safety headroom for concurrent batch requests and CUDA graph generation.1 VRAM VERIFIED.

## **Findings Table**

| \# | Line(s) | Section | Severity | Finding | Evidence |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | N/A | Ingestion Pipeline | 💀 CRITICAL | Invalid do\_ocr=True API usage in Docling initialization. | 4 DocumentConverter does not accept do\_ocr directly. It must be nested in PdfPipelineOptions(do\_ocr=True). |
| 2 | N/A | Retrieval Pipeline | 💀 CRITICAL | Dynamic thresholding (40% of top score) mathematically invalidates Qdrant Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF). | 6 RRF scores are rank-based sums (![][image1]). A percentage threshold on a non-linear rank scale causes unpredictable context truncation. |
| 3 | N/A | Embedding & Indexing | 💀 CRITICAL | Applying Qdrant's modifier="idf" to BGE-M3 sparse vectors corrupts the embedding space. | 8 BGE-M3 generates learned lexical weights (SPLADE-like). Applying BM25 IDF double-weights the tokens, destroying retrieval accuracy. |
| 4 | N/A | Ingestion Pipeline | 💀 CRITICAL | Chunk metadata mapping fails to extract PDF page numbers from Docling HybridChunker. | 10 Page numbers are nested in chunk.meta.doc\_items\[i\].prov\[j\].page\_no. Flat calls to chunk.meta.page\_no return null. |
| 5 | N/A | Inference Layer | ⚠️ SIGNIFICANT | Sub-optimal AWQ kernel deployment limits inference throughput. | 11 vLLM defaults to unoptimized kernels for AWQ. Must explicitly use \--quantization awq\_marlin for maximum performance. |
| 6 | N/A | Security | ⚠️ SIGNIFICANT | Air-gap violation via unconfigured AutoTokenizer and Docling models. | 13 Without HF\_HUB\_OFFLINE=1 and local\_files\_only=True, the Python pipeline will attempt external network calls to HuggingFace Hub at launch. |
| 7 | N/A | Docker Compose | 🔍 MINOR | Docker Compose json-file log rotation parameters require string encapsulation. | 15 max-size: 10m and max-file: 3 will throw parsing errors if passed as integers without quotes in the daemon/compose YAML. |
| 8 | N/A | Inference Layer | 🔍 MINOR | vLLM missing memory defragmentation environment variable for long context. | 3 PYTORCH\_CUDA\_ALLOC\_CONF=expandable\_segments:True is required in the environment to prevent CUDA OOM fragmentation at 32K context. |

## **Heritage Verification**

| V9 Component | Preserved in V10? | Accurate? | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Gus DAG State Machine | ✅ | ❌ | The core state machine (PHASE\_A → B → C → D) is preserved, but the transition logic relies on V9's AnythingLLM citation formats. V10 uses Qdrant, meaning the system prompt must explicitly instruct the LLM to cite payload.filename and payload.page\_no instead of sources.title.10 |
| parseGusResponse() | ✅ | ✅ | Logic correctly anticipates the deterministic JSON output mandated by the temperature 0.1 setting. DOMPurify integration remains intact to sanitize the HTML output payload before React rendering. |
| buildUserMessage() | ✅ | ✅ | Correctly prepends the stringified Qdrant context payload to the user's explicit query, ensuring the strict separation of system instructions and user-provided RAG data. |
| Security Measures | ✅ | ❌ | UFW, Nginx TLS, and localhost binding are preserved. However, the V9 architecture relied on Open WebUI's internal RBAC. The custom React frontend in V10 lacks documentation on JWT/Session validation for the API endpoints. |
| VMDK/OVA Extraction | ✅ | ✅ | The standalone Python daemon utilizing guestfs and tarfile is preserved flawlessly. The output paths correctly align with the new Docling ingestion queue directories. |
| Tribal Knowledge Ledger | ✅ | ✅ | validate\_ledger.py and sync\_ledger.py are accurately referenced. The shift from tiktoken to AutoTokenizer for budget calculations in the sync script is correctly documented.14 |
| 49 Audit Findings | ✅ | ✅ | V9 findings, particularly TOCTOU file-locking mechanisms and the elimination of unpkg.com for PDF.js, are properly integrated into the V10 operational constraints.19 |

## **NotebookLM Parity Check**

| NotebookLM Finding | V10 Implementation | Gap? |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Dual-track ingestion | Docling DocumentConverter generates a semantic markdown representation for the LLM while the frontend retains the native PDF via PDF.js for visual rendering.19 | No gap. The architecture correctly severs the semantic representation from the visual representation, achieving exact parity with the Intel benchmark. |
| Structural segmentation | HybridChunker operates over DocLayNet bounding boxes, preserving table matrices and header hierarchies prior to token-based chunking.5 | Minor gap. Docling's HybridChunker resolves table spanning, but complex multi-page tables will still fragment unless TableFormerMode.ACCURATE is explicitly enforced in the pipeline configuration.5 |
| Greedy token-capped context | The retrieval layer pulls Qdrant payloads until the 27,868 token budget is exhausted, concatenating them linearly. | Critical gap. The RRF dynamic thresholding logic (Finding 2\) will trigger a premature context cutoff, preventing the greedy algorithm from maximizing the token budget.6 |

## **Audit Checklist Results**

### **A. Component Map**

**FAIL.** While the component map represents all five fundamental containers (vLLM, Text Embeddings Inference, Qdrant, GusEngine, and the Custom React Frontend) and logically validates the volume mounts, it exhibits a critical omission regarding operational environment variables required to enforce the strict air-gap boundary. Without explicitly injecting HF\_HUB\_OFFLINE=1 into both the TEI and vLLM container environments, the underlying transformers library will attempt outbound HTTPS requests to huggingface.co/api/models/ to verify model SHAs and download remote code execution files at launch.13 In an isolated, life-safety-adjacent automotive diagnostic network, these outbound calls will result in a connection timeout, plunging the inference containers into an unrecoverable crash loop. Furthermore, the frontend configuration map must guarantee that static assets, specifically the PDF.js worker threads required for dual-track document viewing, are served exclusively from the local Nginx volume rather than defaulting to public CDNs such as unpkg.com or cdnjs, a legacy configuration vulnerability flagged in the V9 architecture but requiring explicit enforcement in the V10 Dockerfile.19

### **B. GPU Memory Budget**

**PASS.** The independent VRAM calculations conclusively substantiate the V10 architectural claims. The target model, Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct, when compressed via 4-bit Activation-aware Weight Quantization (AWQ), occupies approximately 16.25 GB of VRAM.1 The 32,768-token context window, optimized through the model's Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) architecture utilizing 8 KV heads and a 128-head dimension, mathematically necessitates 8.59 GB of continuous memory allocation in FP16 precision.1 Aggregating the infrastructural overhead of the vLLM execution engine, the TEI BGE-M3 embedding container, and conservative CUDA graph allocations brings the total operational footprint to approximately 29.34 GB. This configuration comfortably resides within the bounds of a 48 GB environment, preserving over 18 GB of safety headroom. This extensive buffer is mandatory to mitigate dynamic fragmentation spikes during high-throughput parallel sequence generation. The architecture appropriately restricts claims to the 32K context boundary, avoiding the marketing-driven 128K context claims that would aggressively breach the 48 GB threshold without implementing multi-node tensor parallelism.3

### **C. Docker Compose**

**FAIL.** The Docker Compose specification contains dual syntax and configuration flaws that will interrupt deployment. First, the log rotation implementation utilizes raw integers (max-size: 10m, max-file: 3), a formatting choice that violates the strict string-parsing constraints of the Docker daemon's json-file logging driver.15 The daemon will fail to parse the YAML unless these values are explicitly enclosed in quotes. Second, and more critically, the vLLM container definition omits the PYTORCH\_CUDA\_ALLOC\_CONF=expandable\_segments:True environment variable.3 At context lengths approaching 32,768 tokens, PyTorch's native memory allocator is highly susceptible to CUDA Out-Of-Memory (OOM) errors caused by page fragmentation, even when sufficient contiguous raw VRAM is technically available on the physical hardware. Furthermore, the depends\_on orchestration mapping must implement condition: service\_healthy rather than default startup checks; the Qdrant vector store and vLLM engine require extended initialization and weight-loading phases before the GusEngine API is permitted to initiate TCP binding requests.

### **D. Ingestion Pipeline**

**FAIL.** The Docling integration strategy harbors severe API misuse and object traversal errors that fundamentally compromise the document ingestion sequence. The V10 architecture instructs the deployment of Docling using the syntax DocumentConverter(do\_ocr=True). This argument passing methodology is deprecated and inherently invalid in the Docling v2.x Python API. Optical Character Recognition parameters must be instantiated within a dedicated PdfPipelineOptions class and subsequently passed hierarchically through the PdfFormatOption dictionary.4 Attempting the flat argument syntax will trigger an immediate Pydantic validation error, terminating the extraction daemon. Additionally, the architecture's reliance on chunk.meta.page\_no to retrieve document provenance data is mathematically flawed. Docling's HybridChunker operates over semantic boundaries that frequently span page breaks; therefore, page metadata is deeply nested within a complex provenance tree (chunk.meta.doc\_items.prov.page\_no).10 The current flat-call implementation will inject null values into the Qdrant payload, permanently disabling the frontend user interface's ability to navigate the technician to the correct schematic page within the PDF viewer.

### **E. Embedding & Indexing**

**FAIL.** The Qdrant schema design engineered for hybrid search execution is compromised by a fundamental misunderstanding of the chosen BGE-M3 embedding model's neural architecture. BGE-M3 operates as an "all-in-one" embedding engine, simultaneously generating 1024-dimensional dense vectors, ColBERT multi-vectors, and learned sparse vectors.25 Crucially, the sparse vectors generated by BGE-M3 consist of neural lexical weights computed via masked language modeling, directly analogous to SPLADE architectures.8 The V10 architecture incorrectly configures the Qdrant sparse vector collection with modifier="idf". This applies an algorithmic BM25 Inverse Document Frequency (IDF) weighting.6 Applying a statistical IDF algorithm on top of pre-computed neural lexical weights results in a catastrophic double-weighting error. This mathematical corruption distorts the vector geometry, heavily penalizing dense, domain-specific automotive terminology and effectively destroying the exact-match retrieval capabilities mandated for locating alphanumeric diagnostic trouble codes (DTCs) and component part numbers. The Qdrant sparse collection must be initialized with the modifier explicitly disabled.

### **F. Retrieval Pipeline**

**FAIL.** The hybrid retrieval logic introduces a mathematical fallacy by applying linear percentage thresholding to Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) scores. To fulfill the "Greedy token-capped context injection" requirement, the architecture proposes establishing a dynamic cutoff threshold calculated as "40% of the maximum retrieved score." This logic demonstrates a profound misunderstanding of Qdrant's fusion mechanics.6 RRF does not measure semantic distance or vector cosine similarity; it computes a harmonic series summation based strictly on document rank positions (![][image1]).6 Consequently, the RRF score distribution curve is exceptionally flat among the top results before decaying precipitously. A 40% numerical reduction from the maximum theoretical RRF score typically occurs within the first three to five ranked documents. Applying this percentage-based threshold will act as an arbitrary guillotine, prematurely terminating the context retrieval phase regardless of the underlying semantic relevance of the subsequent documents. This failure mechanism will systematically starve the 32K context window, directly violating the architectural mandate to maximize token injection.

### **G. Inference Layer**

**FAIL.** The vLLM command-line configuration for deploying the Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct-AWQ model lacks essential parameters required for deployment stability and acceptable token throughput. The architecture specifies standard AWQ quantization execution. However, vLLM's standard AWQ kernel implementation suffers from profound computational bottlenecks on modern NVIDIA Ampere, Ada Lovelace, and Hopper architectures. The vllm serve command must be explicitly appended with the \--quantization awq\_marlin flag.11 The Marlin kernels re-optimize weight-only quantization matrix multiplication operations, yielding a 2x to 3x increase in token generation speed. For an automotive diagnostic engine operating on massive 32K context windows where Time-To-First-Token (TTFT) dictates user experience, the omission of the Marlin kernel configuration is an unacceptable architectural oversight. Furthermore, AWQ deployment necessitates the strict enforcement of the \--dtype float16 parameter. Omitting this declaration forces vLLM to rely on automatic datatype inference, which frequently defaults to bfloat16 for the Qwen model family; executing AWQ quantized matrices in bfloat16 triggers immediate tensor mismatch exceptions and catastrophic container termination.3

### **H. System Prompt (Gus DAG V10)**

**PASS.** The Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) state machine has been flawlessly ported from the V9 architecture, preserving the rigorous logic enforcement of Phase A (Initial Triage) through Phase D (Resolution Formulation). The critical RETRIEVAL\_FAILURE safeguard remains fully intact, explicitly instructing the LLM to halt diagnostic generation and request human intervention when the Qdrant database returns an empty context payload, thereby eliminating hallucination vectors. The citation logic has been appropriately modified to align with Qdrant's strict JSON payload structure, migrating away from the deprecated AnythingLLM array formatting. The mandated inference temperature of 0.1 remains highly optimal, ensuring deterministic traversal of the decision nodes and the reliable output of strictly formatted JSON objects for frontend parsing.

### **I. Frontend Architecture**

**PASS.** The architectural contract mapping the V9 CSS class structures to the custom React framework demonstrates exact heritage preservation. The explicit constraint to entirely self-host the PDF.js library resolves the critical air-gap vulnerabilities identified in previous iterations where the system attempted to pull worker threads from external CDNs. The execution of the parseGusResponse() handler successfully intercepts the deterministic JSON stream originating from the vLLM backend, applies the DOMPurify library to strip potential cross-site scripting (XSS) vectors that could be maliciously injected through the tribal knowledge ledger, and reliably renders the diagnostic output alongside the native document viewer.

### **J. Tribal Knowledge**

**PASS.** The ledger injection mechanism, engineered to execute direct FastAPI file read operations to bypass the embedding layers for explicitly defined institutional knowledge, is accurately updated. The transition from utilizing OpenAI's proprietary tiktoken library to HuggingFace's open-source AutoTokenizer ensures that the context window budget is mathematically calibrated against Qwen2.5's native 151,643-token vocabulary.1 This migration eliminates the 15-20% token miscalculation variance observed during the V9 lifecycle, which relied on the incompatible cl100k\_base standard for non-OpenAI models.

### **K. Security**

**PASS.** The internal networking topology strictly adheres to the mandated air-gap security posture. The absolute enforcement of localhost binding (127.0.0.1) on the vLLM (Port 8000), TEI (Port 8080), and Qdrant (Port 6333\) container interfaces guarantees that the databases and inference engines are electromagnetically isolated from the external network bridge. The Uncomplicated Firewall (UFW) rulesets and the Nginx reverse proxy configurations maintain absolute control over all ingress pathways, restricting traffic exclusively to the authenticated frontend application.

### **L. Verification**

**PASS.** The 15-point verification checklist is logically sequenced and practically executable within a deployment scenario. The inclusion of a mandatory, physical air-gap verification step—requiring the disconnection of the external network interface prior to initiating the docker compose up command sequence—represents the definitive functional test of the architecture's self-reliance. The GPU verification parameters accurately map to anticipated nvidia-smi readouts to continuously monitor and confirm the \~29.34 GB operational allocation footprint.

### **M. Known Boundaries**

**PASS.** The architecture document exercises commendable engineering restraint, deliberately avoiding the pervasive industry anti-pattern of overclaiming Large Language Model capabilities. By explicitly documenting the boundaries of the natively supported 32K context window and refusing to implement dynamic YaRN (Yet another RoPE extensioN) scaling—a technique that mathematically extends the context length to 128K but measurably degrades short-context reasoning and MMLU benchmark performance 24—the architects have appropriately prioritized determinism and diagnostic reliability over inflated marketing metrics. The prescribed upgrade pathways for larger hardware configurations are realistic and mathematically sound.

## **Summary Statistics**

| Metric | Count |
| :---- | :---- |
| 💀 CRITICAL findings | 4 |
| ⚠️ SIGNIFICANT findings | 2 |
| 🔍 MINOR findings | 2 |
| Total lines audited | 1,123 |
| Heritage items verified | 7/7 |
| NotebookLM parity items | 3/3 |

## **Overall Verdict**

**VERDICT: BLOCKED**

The V10 architecture is conceptually sophisticated but mathematically and programmatically flawed at the execution layer. The critical failures residing within Docling's pipeline configuration (F1, F4), Qdrant's sparse vector statistical weighting (F3), and the RRF dynamic thresholding logic (F2) guarantee immediate code execution exceptions and the severe corruption of the retrieval context. The architecture cannot proceed to production deployment until the ingestion scripts and vector database query schemas are fundamentally rewritten to correct these API misuse errors.

## ---

**Deep-Dive Architectural Cross-Examination**

To strictly adhere to the hostile auditing mandate and provide exhaustive evidence for the findings synthesized above, the following section dissects the catastrophic failure points identified within the ARCHITECTURE\_V10.md implementation plan. The analysis explores the theoretical mechanics, programmatic implementations, and cascading failure paths associated with the identified errors.

### **Subsystem Alpha: The Ingestion Pipeline Catastrophe (Docling)**

The architectural intent to achieve structural parity with the Intel NotebookLM benchmark relies entirely on the successful deployment of the Docling hybrid chunking library. This tool leverages advanced vision-language models to perform layout analysis (DocLayNet) and table structure recognition (TableFormer) prior to executing token-based segmentation.5 However, the V10 programmatic implementation guarantees a terminal pipeline crash due to fundamental misunderstandings of the Docling v2.x Python API.

**The do\_ocr Initialization Failure (Finding 1\)**

The architecture commands the deployment of the Docling ingestion engine using the syntax DocumentConverter(do\_ocr=True). This syntax is deprecated, invalid, and incompatible with the current Docling data model. The DocumentConverter class acts as an overarching orchestration layer; it does not directly ingest processing flags. Attempting to pass processing parameters directly to the root converter object triggers an immediate ValidationError via Pydantic, halting the execution thread.

The integration of Optical Character Recognition (OCR)—vital for extracting text from scanned automotive schematics—requires a strict hierarchical configuration. The application must instantiate a PdfPipelineOptions class, enable the OCR flag internally, and subsequently bind this customized pipeline to the PDF input format enumerator:

Python

from docling.datamodel.pipeline\_options import PdfPipelineOptions  
from docling.document\_converter import DocumentConverter, PdfFormatOption  
from docling.datamodel.base\_models import InputFormat

pipeline\_options \= PdfPipelineOptions(do\_ocr=True)  
doc\_converter \= DocumentConverter(  
    format\_options={InputFormat.PDF: PdfFormatOption(pipeline\_options=pipeline\_options)}  
)

Failure to rectify this initialization syntax will result in the ingestion daemon crashing during the processing of the very first uploaded diagnostic manual, completely halting the entire Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) lifecycle before vectors can be generated.4

**The Provenance Metadata Collapse (Finding 4\)**

The core objective of the tribal knowledge RAG system is to direct human technicians to the exact page of a 3,000-page service manual for verification. V10 instructs the pipeline to capture this locational data by extracting the chunk.meta.page\_no attribute. This indicates a fatal misunderstanding of how the HybridChunker constructs data over the unified DoclingDocument representation.

A "chunk" generated by the HybridChunker is not a flat string of characters; it is a logically stitched aggregation of multiple layout items—including paragraphs, list items, and table cells. Because a single semantic chunk may physically span across a document page break, Docling cannot assign a single, flat page number to the parent object. Instead, Docling preserves locational fidelity by storing provenance tracking data inside an array of doc\_items. Each item within that array contains its own nested array of prov (provenance) objects, which finally hold the page\_no integer and bounding box coordinates.10

![][image2]

To successfully associate a semantic chunk with its origin page, the parsing daemon must execute a deeply nested traversal logic algorithm:

Python

page\_numbers \= sorted(  
    set(  
        prov.page\_no   
        for item in chunk.meta.doc\_items   
        for prov in getattr(item, "prov",)   
        if hasattr(prov, "page\_no")  
    )  
)

Relying on the flat metadata call specified in the V10 architecture document will yield null responses. This error will propagate downstream, resulting in Qdrant payloads devoid of page numbers, thereby permanently severing the semantic text representation from its visual location in the self-hosted PDF.js viewer.

### **Subsystem Beta: Vector Math and Fusion Corruption (Qdrant)**

The engineering decision documented in the DNA file to transition from LanceDB to Qdrant was primarily predicated on Qdrant's native execution of Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and sophisticated hybrid search operations. However, the V10 architectural configuration fundamentally corrupts the mathematical foundations of both the sparse vector embedding space and the retrieval fusion thresholds.

**The Sparse Vector Double-Weighting Error (Finding 3\)** The architecture specifies the deployment of the BAAI BGE-M3 model for embedding generation. BGE-M3 is an advanced "all-in-one" embedding engine that simultaneously generates three distinct vector representations during a single forward pass: 1024-dimensional dense vectors for semantic similarity, ColBERT multi-vectors for fine-grained token matching, and learned sparse vectors for precise lexical retrieval.25

The critical distinction lies in the term *learned* sparse vectors. Traditional sparse retrieval algorithms, such as BM25, calculate the relevance of documents based on raw token frequencies, applying an Inverse Document Frequency (IDF) modifier to penalize overly common terms (like "the" or "and").8 BGE-M3, conversely, does not rely on raw statistical frequencies. It utilizes a Masked Language Modeling (MLM) neural architecture—conceptually similar to SPLADE—to calculate contextual, floating-point lexical weights based on deep semantic understanding.8

Despite this, V10 dictates configuring the Qdrant sparse vector collection with modifier="idf".6 The IDF modifier is Qdrant's internal mechanism for calculating BM25 statistical scores on the fly for raw integer text frequencies. Applying Qdrant's rigid statistical IDF algorithm to BGE-M3's highly optimized, floating-point neural lexical weights constitutes a catastrophic double-weighting error. It mathematically distorts the entire vector geometry, heavily penalizing dense domain-specific terminology that may appear frequently within a specific technical manual, but retains critical diagnostic importance. This configuration destroys the precise keyword matching capabilities required for retrieving complex alphanumeric automotive diagnostic codes and must be disabled by setting the modifier to None.30

**The Mathematical Fallacy of RRF Thresholding (Finding 2\)**

In a misguided attempt to fulfill the "Greedy token-capped context injection" requirement defined by the NotebookLM benchmark, the V10 design implements a dynamic threshold to slice off irrelevant results from the final fused query. The logic explicitly defined in the architecture is to establish a cutoff at "40% of the top score".

This operational logic exposes a critical lack of mathematical comprehension regarding how Qdrant fuses distinct retrieval sets. Reciprocal Rank Fusion does not measure vector distance, dot products, or cosine similarity. It is a purely rank-based aggregation algorithm. The RRF score is calculated as the summation of the reciprocal of a document's rank across multiple retrieval pipelines: ![][image3], where Qdrant applies a default constant ![][image4] value of 60\.6

To illustrate the mathematical failure, consider a document that ranks highly across both the dense and sparse pipelines. If Document A ranks \#1 in dense search and \#1 in sparse search, its combined RRF score is:

![][image5]

If Document B ranks \#3 in dense and \#4 in sparse, its combined score is:

![][image6]

![][image7]

The mathematical decay is exceptionally flat at the absolute top of the ranking, yet steepens aggressively. A 40% reduction from the maximum theoretical score of 0.0327 establishes a cutoff threshold of 0.0196. Because a document ranking \#1 in one pipeline but \#60 in another yields an RRF score of only 0.0247, the 0.0196 threshold acts as an arbitrary guillotine. It severs the context pipeline almost immediately, regardless of the deep semantic relevance of the subsequent documents. By thresholding a rank-based harmonic metric using a flat percentage, the RAG engine will consistently and arbitrarily drop highly relevant context chunks, severely restricting the 32K context window capabilities of the vLLM engine. The thresholding logic must be transitioned to evaluate the underlying dense cosine similarity scores prior to fusion, or alternatively, the greedy context builder must abandon score thresholding entirely and rely strictly on a hard token-count limit.

### **Subsystem Gamma: Inference Engine Constraints (vLLM)**

The strategic migration from deploying 70-billion parameter models down to the 32-billion tier explicitly resolved the crippling VRAM bottlenecks that plagued the V9 iteration. The Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct-AWQ model is perfectly scoped to operate within a 48 GB environment. However, the Docker Compose deployment flags listed in the V10 documentation will inevitably induce severe performance degradation, resulting in thermal throttling, CUDA allocation panics, and a total collapse of token generation throughput.

**The Marlin Kernel Imperative (Finding 5\)** The V10 architecture dictates the initiation of the vLLM container utilizing the standard AWQ quantization flag (--quantization awq). While technically functional, vLLM's standard AWQ kernel implementations suffer from profound computational bottlenecks on modern NVIDIA hardware, specifically Ampere, Ada Lovelace, and Hopper microarchitectures.12 The standard AWQ kernels fail to effectively utilize the hardware's tensor cores during weight-only quantization decoding.

To achieve production-grade latency, the vllm serve execution command must be strictly appended with the \--quantization awq\_marlin flag.3 The Marlin kernels act as a highly optimized backend, dynamically rearranging the quantized model weights in VRAM to align perfectly with the matrix multiplication thread tiles of the underlying GPU. This low-level optimization yields a measured 2x to 3x increase in token generation speed over standard AWQ. For an automotive diagnostic system operating on extensive 32,768-token context windows, where Time-To-First-Token (TTFT) metrics dictate the operational viability of the tool for mechanics on the shop floor, the omission of the Marlin kernel configuration is an unacceptable architectural oversight. Furthermore, AWQ deployment strictly requires the inclusion of the \--dtype float16 parameter. Omitting this declaration forces the vLLM engine to rely on automatic datatype inference, which systematically defaults to bfloat16 for the Qwen model lineage; executing 4-bit AWQ quantized matrices within a bfloat16 execution scope triggers immediate tensor mismatch exceptions and catastrophic container termination.3

**Memory Fragmentation at 32K Context (Finding 8\)**

While the theoretical KV cache mathematical allocations (8.59 GB) comfortably fit within the gross VRAM budget, sustained conversational interactions operating at the 32,768-token boundary induce rapid and severe memory fragmentation within PyTorch's backend allocation pools. Over continuous hours of operation, as the vLLM engine dynamically allocates and frees blocks of memory via its PagedAttention mechanism to serve concurrent diagnostic requests, the physical VRAM becomes fragmented into non-contiguous segments.

Without targeted intervention at the environment level, this fragmentation inevitably results in a CUDA Out-Of-Memory (OOM) crash, despite the host system's nvidia-smi telemetry indicating that gigabytes of memory remain technically available. To mitigate this known architectural vulnerability, the vLLM Docker Compose environment configuration must be patched to include the PYTORCH\_CUDA\_ALLOC\_CONF=expandable\_segments:True directive.16 This specific parameter forces the PyTorch memory allocator to dynamically defragment active memory blocks and expand underlying segment sizes during runtime, preserving the availability of contiguous memory blocks and ensuring maximum uptime for the diagnostic inference engine.

### **Subsystem Delta: Air-Gap and Network Boundary Violations**

The architectural intent mandates a strict, unyielding air-gap for the inference layers, ensuring that the life-safety diagnostic system remains entirely disconnected from the broader internet. While the network topology (ports, reverse proxying, UFW firewalling) adheres to this constraint, the software application layer harbors critical vulnerabilities that violate the air-gap perimeter.

**The AutoTokenizer Hub Dependency (Finding 6\)** The tribal knowledge ingestion script utilizes the HuggingFace AutoTokenizer library to accurately calculate context token budgets prior to vector database injection. The V10 architecture specifies the initialization of this component via AutoTokenizer.from\_pretrained(). By default, this function initiates an outbound HTTPS sequence, attempting to contact huggingface.co/api/models/ to verify the presence of updated model weights, retrieve repository JSON configurations, and validate execution code.13

Within the mandated offline environment, these outbound connections will be actively dropped by the network bridge, resulting in Python TimeoutError exceptions that crash the tribal knowledge synchronization script. To securely operate within the air-gap, the system must enforce strict offline constraints. The execution environment for the GusEngine and TEI containers must explicitly declare HF\_HUB\_OFFLINE=1 to disable the HuggingFace Hub client API entirely.13 Furthermore, the Python initialization code must be appended with the local\_files\_only=True parameter to strictly bind the tokenizer to the local volume mounts, permanently preventing unauthorized external network traversal attempts.

#### **Works cited**

1. Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct \- Hugging Face, accessed February 24, 2026, [https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct)  
2. Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct-AWQ \- Hugging Face, accessed February 24, 2026, [https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct-AWQ](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct-AWQ)  
3. Proper settings for running qwen2.5 72b on 48gb using awq \- \#2 by RunLLM \- vLLM Forums, accessed February 24, 2026, [https://discuss.vllm.ai/t/proper-settings-for-running-qwen2-5-72b-on-48gb-using-awq/885/2](https://discuss.vllm.ai/t/proper-settings-for-running-qwen2-5-72b-on-48gb-using-awq/885/2)  
4. are there any specific settings that i have to enable inorder to get the text from images? give me a code snippet · Issue \#2070 · docling-project/docling \- GitHub, accessed February 24, 2026, [https://github.com/docling-project/docling/issues/2070](https://github.com/docling-project/docling/issues/2070)  
5. docling 1.16.1 \- PyPI, accessed February 24, 2026, [https://pypi.org/project/docling/1.16.1/](https://pypi.org/project/docling/1.16.1/)  
6. Demo: Implementing a Hybrid Search System \- Qdrant, accessed February 24, 2026, [https://qdrant.tech/course/essentials/day-3/hybrid-search-demo/](https://qdrant.tech/course/essentials/day-3/hybrid-search-demo/)
